OrganizationDetails.description: Information about the statistics report for your organization.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp at which the organization statistics was last updated. This is in UTC format.

OrganizationStatistics.description: Information about the GuardDuty coverage statistics for members in your Amazon Web Services organization.

TotalAccountsCount.description: Total number of accounts in your Amazon Web Services organization.

MemberAccountsCount.description: Total number of accounts in your Amazon Web Services organization that are associated with GuardDuty.

ActiveAccountsCount.description: Total number of active accounts in your Amazon Web Services organization that are associated with GuardDuty.

EnabledAccountsCount.description: Total number of accounts that have enabled GuardDuty.

CountByFeature.description: Retrieves the coverage statistics for each feature.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.
The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

AccountId.description: The ID of the account that the invitation was sent from.

InvitationId.description: The ID of the invitation. This value is used to validate the inviter account to the member account.

RelationshipStatus.description: The status of the relationship between the inviter and invitee accounts.

InvitedAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was sent.

MalwareProtectionPlans.description: A list of unique identifiers associated with each Malware Protection plan.

NextToken.description: You can use this parameter when paginating results. Set the value of this parameter to null on your first call to the list action. For subsequent calls to the action, fill nextToken in the request with the value of NextToken from the previous response to continue listing data.

MalwareProtectionPlanId.description: A unique identifier associated with Malware Protection plan.

Scans.description: The list of malware scans associated with the provided input parameters.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more scans.

ResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource for the given malware scan.

ResourceType.description: The type of resource that was scanned for malware.

ScanId.description: A unique identifier that gets generated when you invoke the API without any error. Each malware scan has a corresponding scan ID. Using this scan ID, you can monitor the status of your malware scan.

ScanStatus.description: An enum value representing the current status of the malware scan.

ScanResultStatus.description: An enum value representing the result of the malware scan.

ScanType.description: An enum value representing the type of scan that was initiated.

ScanStartedAt.description: The timestamp representing when the malware scan was started.

ScanCompletedAt.description: The timestamp representing when the malware scan was completed.

AdminAccounts.description: A list of accounts configured as GuardDuty delegated administrators.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

AdminAccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID for the account.

AdminStatus.description: Indicates whether the account is enabled as the delegated administrator.

DetectorId.description: The unique ID of the created detector.

UnprocessedDataSources.description: Specifies the data sources that couldn't be enabled when GuardDuty was enabled for the first time.

MalwareProtection.description: An object that contains information on the status of all Malware Protection data sources.

ScanEc2InstanceWithFindings.description: Describes the configuration of Malware Protection for EC2 instances with findings.

EbsVolumes.description: Describes the configuration of scanning EBS volumes as a data source.

Status.description: Describes whether scanning EBS volumes is enabled as a data source.

Reason.description: Specifies the reason why scanning EBS volumes (Malware Protection) was not enabled as a data source.

Name.description: The name of the successfully created filter.

MalwareProtectionPlanId.description: A unique identifier associated with the Malware Protection plan resource.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that include the accountIds of the unprocessed accounts and a result string that explains why each was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

DestinationId.description: The ID of the publishing destination that is created.

ThreatEntitySetId.description: The ID returned by GuardDuty after creation of the threat entity set resource.

TrustedEntitySetId.description: The ID returned by GuardDuty after creation of the trusted entity set resource.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: The accounts that could not be processed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

Scans.description: Contains information about malware scans associated with GuardDuty Malware Protection for EC2.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

DetectorId.description:
The unique ID of the detector that is associated with the request.

To find the detectorId in the current Region, see the Settings page in the GuardDuty console, or run the ListDetectors API.

AdminDetectorId.description:
The unique detector ID of the administrator account that the request is associated with. If the account is an administrator, the AdminDetectorId will be the same as the one used for DetectorId.

To find the detectorId in the current Region, see the Settings page in the GuardDuty console, or run the ListDetectors API.

ScanId.description: The unique scan ID associated with a scan entry.

ScanStatus.description: An enum value representing possible scan statuses.

ScanStartTime.description: The timestamp of when the scan was triggered.

ScanEndTime.description: The timestamp of when the scan was finished.

TriggerDetails.description: Specifies the reason why the scan was initiated.

GuardDutyFindingId.description: The ID of the GuardDuty finding that triggered the malware scan.

TriggerType.description: Specifies the trigger type that started the malware scan.

ResourceDetails.description: Represents the resources that were scanned in the scan entry.

InstanceArn.description: Instance ARN that was scanned in the scan entry.

ScanResult.description: An enum value representing possible scan results.

AccountId.description: The ID for the account that belongs to the scan.

FileCount.description: Represents the number of files that were scanned.

AttachedVolumes.description: List of volumes that were attached to the original instance to be scanned.

ScanType.description: Specifies the scan type that invoked the malware scan.

AutoEnable.description:
Indicates whether GuardDuty is automatically enabled for accounts added to the organization.

Even though this is still supported, we recommend using AutoEnableOrganizationMembers to achieve the similar results.

MemberAccountLimitReached.description: Indicates whether the maximum number of allowed member accounts are already associated with the delegated administrator account for your organization.

DataSources.description: Describes which data sources are enabled automatically for member accounts.

Features.description: A list of features that are configured for this organization.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

AutoEnableOrganizationMembers.description:
Indicates the auto-enablement configuration of GuardDuty or any of the corresponding protection plans for the member accounts in the organization.

  • NEW: Indicates that when a new account joins the organization, they will have GuardDuty or any of the corresponding protection plans enabled automatically.

  • ALL: Indicates that all accounts in the organization have GuardDuty and any of the corresponding protection plans enabled automatically. This includes NEW accounts that join the organization and accounts that may have been suspended or removed from the organization in GuardDuty.

  • NONE: Indicates that GuardDuty or any of the corresponding protection plans will not be automatically enabled for any account in the organization. The administrator must manage GuardDuty for each account in the organization individually.

    When you update the auto-enable setting from ALL or NEW to NONE, this action doesn't disable the corresponding option for your existing accounts. This configuration will apply to the new accounts that join the organization. After you update the auto-enable settings, no new account will have the corresponding option as enabled.

Name.description: The name of the feature that is configured for the member accounts within the organization.

AutoEnable.description:
Describes the status of the feature that is configured for the member accounts within the organization.

  • NEW: Indicates that when a new account joins the organization, they will have the feature enabled automatically.

  • ALL: Indicates that all accounts in the organization have the feature enabled automatically. This includes NEW accounts that join the organization and accounts that may have been suspended or removed from the organization in GuardDuty.

  • NONE: Indicates that the feature will not be automatically enabled for any account in the organization. In this case, each account will be managed individually by the administrator.

AdditionalConfiguration.description: The additional configuration that is configured for the member accounts within the organization.

DestinationType.description: The type of publishing destination. Currently, only Amazon S3 buckets are supported.

PublishingFailureStartTimestamp.description: The time, in epoch millisecond format, at which GuardDuty was first unable to publish findings to the destination.

DestinationProperties.description: A DestinationProperties object that includes the DestinationArn and KmsKeyArn of the publishing destination.

Tags.description: The tags of the publishing destination resource.

DestinationArn.description:
The ARN of the resource to publish to.

To specify an S3 bucket folder use the following format: arn:aws:s3:::DOC-EXAMPLE-BUCKET/myFolder/

KmsKeyArn.description: The ARN of the KMS key to use for encryption.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

AccountId.description: The ID of the account used as the administrator account.

InvitationId.description: The value that is used to validate the administrator account to the member account.

RelationshipStatus.description: The status of the relationship between the administrator and member accounts.

InvitedAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was sent.

CoverageStatistics.description: Represents the count aggregated by the statusCode and resourceType.

CountByResourceType.description: Represents coverage statistics for EKS clusters aggregated by resource type.

CountByCoverageStatus.description: Represents coverage statistics for EKS clusters aggregated by coverage status.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp of when the detector was created.

DataSources.description: Describes which data sources are enabled for the detector.

Features.description: Describes the features that have been enabled for the detector.

Name.description: Indicates the name of the feature that can be enabled for the detector.

Status.description: Indicates the status of the feature that is enabled for the detector.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp at which the feature object was updated.

Action.description: Specifies the action that is to be applied to the findings that match the filter.

Rank.description: Specifies the position of the filter in the list of current filters. Also specifies the order in which this filter is applied to the findings.

FindingCriteria.description: Represents the criteria to be used in the filter for querying findings.

Criterion.description: Represents a map of finding properties that match specified conditions and values when querying findings.

AccountId.description: The ID of the account in which the finding was generated.

CreatedAt.description: The time and date when the finding was created.

Region.description: The Region where the finding was generated. For findings generated from Global Service Events, the Region value in the finding might differ from the Region where GuardDuty identifies the potential threat. For more information, see How GuardDuty handles Amazon Web Services CloudTrail global events in the Amazon GuardDuty User Guide.

Resource.description: Contains information about the Amazon Web Services resource associated with the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate a finding.

AccessKeyDetails.description: The IAM access key details (user information) of a user that engaged in the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate a finding.

AccessKeyId.description: The access key ID of the user.

InstanceDetails.description: The information about the EC2 instance associated with the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate a finding.

AvailabilityZone.description: The Availability Zone of the EC2 instance.

IamInstanceProfile.description: The profile information of the EC2 instance.

Arn.description: The profile ARN of the EC2 instance.

Id.description: The profile ID of the EC2 instance.

ImageDescription.description: The image description of the EC2 instance.

ImageId.description: The image ID of the EC2 instance.

OutpostArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Amazon Web Services Outpost. Only applicable to Amazon Web Services Outposts instances.

LaunchTime.description: The launch time of the EC2 instance.

NetworkInterfaces.description: The elastic network interface information of the EC2 instance.

ProductCodes.description: The product code of the EC2 instance.

EksClusterDetails.description: Details about the EKS cluster involved in a Kubernetes finding.

VpcId.description: The VPC ID to which the EKS cluster is attached.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp when the EKS cluster was created.

KubernetesDetails.description: Details about the Kubernetes user and workload involved in a Kubernetes finding.

KubernetesUserDetails.description: Details about the Kubernetes user involved in a Kubernetes finding.

Username.description: The username of the user who called the Kubernetes API.

Uid.description: The user ID of the user who called the Kubernetes API.

Groups.description: The groups that include the user who called the Kubernetes API.

SessionName.description: Entity that assumes the IAM role when Kubernetes RBAC permissions are assigned to that role.

KubernetesWorkloadDetails.description: Details about the Kubernetes workload involved in a Kubernetes finding.

Namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace that the workload is part of.

HostNetwork.description: Whether the hostNetwork flag is enabled for the pods included in the workload.

Containers.description: Containers running as part of the Kubernetes workload.

ServiceAccountName.description: The service account name that is associated with a Kubernetes workload.

HostIPC.description: Whether the host IPC flag is enabled for the pods in the workload.

HostPID.description: Whether the host PID flag is enabled for the pods in the workload.

ResourceType.description: The type of Amazon Web Services resource.

EbsVolumeDetails.description: Contains list of scanned and skipped EBS volumes with details.

ScannedVolumeDetails.description: List of EBS volumes that were scanned.

SkippedVolumeDetails.description: List of EBS volumes that were skipped from the malware scan.

EcsClusterDetails.description: Contains information about the details of the ECS Cluster.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the cluster.

ActiveServicesCount.description: The number of services that are running on the cluster in an ACTIVE state.

RegisteredContainerInstancesCount.description: The number of container instances registered into the cluster.

RunningTasksCount.description: The number of tasks in the cluster that are in the RUNNING state.

TaskDetails.description: Contains information about the details of the ECS Task.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the task.

DefinitionArn.description: The ARN of the task definition that creates the task.

TaskCreatedAt.description: The Unix timestamp for the time when the task was created.

StartedAt.description: The Unix timestamp for the time when the task started.

StartedBy.description: Contains the tag specified when a task is started.

Volumes.description: The list of data volume definitions for the task.

Containers.description: The containers that's associated with the task.

Group.description: The name of the task group that's associated with the task.

LaunchType.description: A capacity on which the task is running. For example, Fargate and EC2.

ContainerRuntime.description: The container runtime (such as, Docker or containerd) used to run the container.

ImagePrefix.description: Part of the image name before the last slash. For example, imagePrefix for public.ecr.aws/amazonlinux/amazonlinux:latest would be public.ecr.aws/amazonlinux. If the image name is relative and does not have a slash, this field is empty.

AllowPrivilegeEscalation.description: Whether or not a container or a Kubernetes pod is allowed to gain more privileges than its parent process.

RdsDbInstanceDetails.description: Contains information about the database instance to which an anomalous login attempt was made.

DbInstanceIdentifier.description: The identifier associated to the database instance that was involved in the finding.

Engine.description: The database engine of the database instance involved in the finding.

EngineVersion.description: The version of the database engine that was involved in the finding.

DbClusterIdentifier.description: The identifier of the database cluster that contains the database instance ID involved in the finding.

DbInstanceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the database instance involved in the finding.

RdsLimitlessDbDetails.description: Contains information about the RDS Limitless database that was involved in a GuardDuty finding.

DbShardGroupIdentifier.description: The name associated with the Limitless DB shard group.

DbShardGroupResourceId.description: The resource identifier of the DB shard group within the Limitless Database.

DbShardGroupArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the DB shard group.

Engine.description: The database engine of the database instance involved in the finding.

DbClusterIdentifier.description: The name of the database cluster that is a part of the Limitless Database.

RdsDbUserDetails.description: Contains information about the user details through which anomalous login attempt was made.

User.description: The user name used in the anomalous login attempt.

Application.description: The application name used in the anomalous login attempt.

Database.description: The name of the database instance involved in the anomalous login attempt.

Ssl.description: The version of the Secure Socket Layer (SSL) used for the network.

AuthMethod.description: The authentication method used by the user involved in the finding.

LambdaDetails.description: Contains information about the Lambda function that was involved in a finding.

FunctionArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Lambda function.

LastModifiedAt.description: The timestamp when the Lambda function was last modified. This field is in the UTC date string format (2023-03-22T19:37:20.168Z).

RevisionId.description: The revision ID of the Lambda function version.

Role.description: The execution role of the Lambda function.

VpcConfig.description: Amazon Virtual Private Cloud configuration details associated with your Lambda function.

SubnetIds.description: The identifiers of the subnets that are associated with your Lambda function.

VpcId.description: The identifier of the Amazon Virtual Private Cloud.

SecurityGroups.description: The identifier of the security group attached to the Lambda function.

Tags.description: A list of tags attached to this resource, listed in the format of key:value pair.

EbsSnapshotDetails.description: Contains details about the EBS snapshot that was scanned.

SnapshotArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the EBS snapshot.

Ec2ImageDetails.description: Contains details about the EC2 image that was scanned.

ImageArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the EC2 AMI.

RecoveryPointDetails.description: Contains details about the backup recovery point that was scanned.

RecoveryPointArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the recovery point.

BackupVaultName.description: The name of the backup vault containing the recovery point.

SchemaVersion.description: The version of the schema used for the finding.

Service.description: Contains additional information about the generated finding.

Action.description: Information about the activity that is described in a finding.

AwsApiCallAction.description: Information about the AWS_API_CALL action described in this finding.

CallerType.description: The Amazon Web Services API caller type.

DomainDetails.description: The domain information for the Amazon Web Services API call.

ErrorCode.description: The error code of the failed Amazon Web Services API action.

UserAgent.description: The agent through which the API request was made.

RemoteIpDetails.description: The remote IP information of the connection that initiated the Amazon Web Services API call.

ServiceName.description: The Amazon Web Services service name whose API was invoked.

RemoteAccountDetails.description: The details of the Amazon Web Services account that made the API call. This field appears if the call was made from outside your account.

AffectedResources.description: The details of the Amazon Web Services account that made the API call. This field identifies the resources that were affected by this API call.

DnsRequestAction.description: Information about the DNS_REQUEST action described in this finding.

Domain.description: The domain information for the DNS query.

Protocol.description: The network connection protocol observed in the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate the finding.

Blocked.description: Indicates whether the targeted port is blocked.

DomainWithSuffix.description: The second and top level domain involved in the activity that potentially prompted GuardDuty to generate this finding. For a list of top-level and second-level domains, see public suffix list.

VpcOwnerAccountId.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the VPC through which the DNS request was made.

NetworkConnectionAction.description: Information about the NETWORK_CONNECTION action described in this finding.

Blocked.description: Indicates whether EC2 blocked the network connection to your instance.

LocalPortDetails.description: The local port information of the connection.

LocalIpDetails.description: The local IP information of the connection.

LocalNetworkInterface.description: The EC2 instance's local elastic network interface utilized for the connection.

RemoteIpDetails.description: The remote IP information of the connection.

RemotePortDetails.description: The remote port information of the connection.

PortProbeAction.description: Information about the PORT_PROBE action described in this finding.

Blocked.description: Indicates whether EC2 blocked the port probe to the instance, such as with an ACL.

PortProbeDetails.description: A list of objects related to port probe details.

KubernetesApiCallAction.description: Information about the Kubernetes API call action described in this finding.

SourceIps.description: The IP of the Kubernetes API caller and the IPs of any proxies or load balancers between the caller and the API endpoint.

UserAgent.description: The user agent of the caller of the Kubernetes API.

RemoteIpDetails.description: Contains information about the remote IP address of the connection.

StatusCode.description: The resulting HTTP response code of the Kubernetes API call action.

Parameters.description: Parameters related to the Kubernetes API call action.

Resource.description: The resource component in the Kubernetes API call action.

Subresource.description: The name of the sub-resource in the Kubernetes API call action.

Namespace.description: The name of the namespace where the Kubernetes API call action takes place.

ResourceName.description: The name of the resource in the Kubernetes API call action.

RdsLoginAttemptAction.description: Information about RDS_LOGIN_ATTEMPT action described in this finding.

RemoteIpDetails.description: Contains information about the remote IP address of the connection.

LoginAttributes.description: Indicates the login attributes used in the login attempt.

KubernetesPermissionCheckedDetails.description: Information whether the user has the permission to use a specific Kubernetes API.

Verb.description: The verb component of the Kubernetes API call. For example, when you check whether or not you have the permission to call the CreatePod API, the verb component will be Create.

Resource.description: The Kubernetes resource with which your Kubernetes API call will interact.

Namespace.description: The namespace where the Kubernetes API action will take place.

Allowed.description: Information whether the user has the permission to call the Kubernetes API.

KubernetesRoleBindingDetails.description: Information about the role binding that grants the permission defined in a Kubernetes role.

Kind.description: The kind of the role. For role binding, this value will be RoleBinding.

Uid.description: The unique identifier of the role binding.

RoleRefName.description: The name of the role being referenced. This must match the name of the Role or ClusterRole that you want to bind to.

RoleRefKind.description: The type of the role being referenced. This could be either Role or ClusterRole.

KubernetesRoleDetails.description: Information about the Kubernetes role name and role type.

Kind.description: The kind of role. For this API, the value of kind will be Role.

Uid.description: The unique identifier of the Kubernetes role name.

Evidence.description: An evidence object associated with the service.

ThreatIntelligenceDetails.description: A list of threat intelligence details related to the evidence.

Count.description: The total count of the occurrences of this finding type.

DetectorId.description: The detector ID for the GuardDuty service.

EventFirstSeen.description: The first-seen timestamp of the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate this finding.

EventLastSeen.description: The last-seen timestamp of the activity that prompted GuardDuty to generate this finding.

ResourceRole.description: The resource role information for this finding.

ServiceName.description: The name of the Amazon Web Services service (GuardDuty) that generated a finding.

UserFeedback.description: Feedback that was submitted about the finding.

AdditionalInfo.description: Contains additional information about the generated finding.

Value.description: This field specifies the value of the additional information.

Type.description: Describes the type of the additional information.

FeatureName.description: The name of the feature that generated a finding.

EbsVolumeScanDetails.description: Returns details from the malware scan that created a finding.

ScanId.description: Unique Id of the malware scan that generated the finding.

ScanStartedAt.description: Returns the start date and time of the malware scan.

ScanCompletedAt.description: Returns the completion date and time of the malware scan.

TriggerFindingId.description: GuardDuty finding ID that triggered a malware scan.

Sources.description: Contains list of threat intelligence sources used to detect threats.

ScanDetections.description: Contains a complete view providing malware scan result details.

HighestSeverityThreatDetails.description: Details of the highest severity threat detected during malware scan and number of infected files.

ThreatDetectedByName.description: Contains details about identified threats organized by threat name.

ScanType.description: Specifies the scan type that invoked the malware scan.

RuntimeDetails.description: Information about the process and any required context values for a specific finding

ExecutablePath.description: The absolute path of the process executable file.

Pwd.description: The present working directory of the process.

StartTime.description: The time when the process started. This is in UTC format.

Uuid.description: The unique ID assigned to the process by GuardDuty.

ParentUuid.description: The unique ID of the parent process. This ID is assigned to the parent process by GuardDuty.

UserId.description: The unique ID of the user that executed the process.

Euid.description: The effective user ID of the user that executed the process.

ModifyingProcess.description: Information about the process that modified the current process. This is available for multiple finding types.

ModifiedAt.description: The timestamp at which the process modified the current process. The timestamp is in UTC date string format.

ScriptPath.description: The path to the script that was executed.

LibraryPath.description: The path to the new library that was loaded.

LdPreloadValue.description: The value of the LD_PRELOAD environment variable.

SocketPath.description: The path to the docket socket that was accessed.

ReleaseAgentPath.description: The path in the container that modified the release agent file.

MountSource.description: The path on the host that is mounted by the container.

MountTarget.description: The path in the container that is mapped to the host directory.

Flags.description: Represents options that control the behavior of a runtime operation or action. For example, a filesystem mount operation may contain a read-only flag.

ModuleName.description: The name of the module loaded into the kernel.

ModuleFilePath.description: The path to the module loaded into the kernel.

ShellHistoryFilePath.description: The path to the modified shell history file.

TargetProcess.description: Information about the process that had its memory overwritten by the current process.

AddressFamily.description: Represents the communication protocol associated with the address. For example, the address family AF_INET is used for IP version of 4 protocol.

IanaProtocolNumber.description: Specifies a particular protocol within the address family. Usually there is a single protocol in address families. For example, the address family AF_INET only has the IP protocol.

MemoryRegions.description: Specifies the Region of a process's address space such as stack and heap.

ToolCategory.description: Category that the tool belongs to. Some of the examples are Backdoor Tool, Pentest Tool, Network Scanner, and Network Sniffer.

ServiceName.description: Name of the security service that has been potentially disabled.

CommandLineExample.description: Example of the command line involved in the suspicious activity.

ThreatFilePath.description: The suspicious file path for which the threat intelligence details were found.

Detection.description: Contains information about the detected unusual behavior.

Anomaly.description: The details about the anomalous activity that caused GuardDuty to generate the finding.

Unusual.description: Information about the behavior of the anomalies.

Actors.description: Contains information about the actors involved in the attack sequence.

Resources.description: Contains information about the resources involved in the attack sequence.

Endpoints.description: Contains information about the network endpoints that were used in the attack sequence.

Signals.description: Contains information about the signals involved in the attack sequence.

SequenceIndicators.description: Contains information about the indicators observed in the attack sequence.

AdditionalSequenceTypes.description: Additional types of sequences that may be associated with the attack sequence finding, providing further context about the nature of the detected threat.

MalwareScanDetails.description: Returns details from the malware scan that generated a GuardDuty finding.

Threats.description: Information about the detected threats associated with the generated GuardDuty finding.

ScanId.description: The unique identifier for the malware scan.

ScanConfiguration.description: The configuration settings used for the malware scan.

TriggerType.description: The event that triggered the malware scan.

IncrementalScanDetails.description: Contains information about the incremental scan configuration.

UniqueThreatCount.description: The number of unique malware threats detected during the scan.

UpdatedAt.description: The time and date when the finding was last updated.

AssociatedAttackSequenceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with the attack sequence finding.

NextToken.description:
The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

This parameter is currently not supported.

CountBySeverity.description: Represents a list of map of severity to count statistics for a set of findings.

GroupedByAccount.description: Represents a list of map of accounts with a findings count associated with each account.

GroupedByDate.description: Represents a list of map of dates with a count of total findings generated on each date per severity level.

GroupedByFindingType.description:
Represents a list of map of finding types with a count of total findings generated for each type.

Based on the orderBy parameter, this request returns either the most occurring finding types or the least occurring finding types. If the orderBy parameter is ASC, this will represent the least occurring finding types in your account; otherwise, this will represent the most occurring finding types. The default value of orderBy is DESC.

GroupedByResource.description: Represents a list of map of top resources with a count of total findings.

GroupedBySeverity.description: Represents a list of map of total findings for each severity level.

Format.description: The format of the file that contains the IPSet.

Location.description: The URI of the file that contains the IPSet.

Status.description: The status of IPSet file that was uploaded.

ExpectedBucketOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the Amazon S3 bucket specified in the location parameter. This field appears in the response only if it was provided during IPSet creation or update.

Arn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the protected resource.

Role.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role that includes the permissions to scan and add tags to the associated protected resource.

ProtectedResource.description: Information about the protected resource that is associated with the created Malware Protection plan. Presently, S3Bucket is the only supported protected resource.

Actions.description: Information about whether the tags will be added to the S3 object after scanning.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp when the Malware Protection plan resource was created.

StatusReasons.description: Information about the issue code and message associated to the status of your Malware Protection plan.

Tags.description: Tags added to the Malware Protection plan resource.

Message.description: Issue message that specifies the reason. For information about potential troubleshooting steps, see Troubleshooting Malware Protection for S3 status issues in the Amazon GuardDuty User Guide.

ScanId.description: A unique identifier associated with the malware scan. Each malware scan has a corresponding scan ID. Using this scan ID, you can monitor the status of your malware scan.

DetectorId.description:
The unique ID of the detector that is associated with the request, if it belongs to an account which is a GuardDuty customer.

To find the detectorId in the current Region, see the Settings page in the GuardDuty console, or run the ListDetectors API.

AdminDetectorId.description:
The unique detector ID of the administrator account that the request is associated with. If the account is an administrator, the AdminDetectorId will be the same as the one used for DetectorId. If the customer is not a GuardDuty customer, this field will not be present..

To find the detectorId in the current Region, see the Settings page in the GuardDuty console, or run the ListDetectors API.

ResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource on which a malware scan was invoked.

ResourceType.description: The type of resource that was scanned for malware.

ScannedResourcesCount.description: The total number of resources that were successfully scanned. This is dependent on the resource type.

SkippedResourcesCount.description: The total number of resources that were skipped during the scan.

FailedResourcesCount.description: The total number of resources that failed to be scanned.

ScannedResources.description: A list of resources along with their metadata that were scanned as part of the malware scan operation.

ScanConfiguration.description: Information about the scan configuration used for the malware scan.

ScanCategory.description: The category of the malware scan, indicating the type of scan performed.

ScanStatus.description: A value representing the current status of the malware scan.

ScanStatusReason.description: Represents the reason for the current scan status, if applicable.

ScanType.description: A value representing the initiator of the scan.

ScanStartedAt.description: The timestamp representing when the malware scan was started.

ScanCompletedAt.description: The timestamp representing when the malware scan was completed.

ScanResultDetails.description: Detailed information about the results of the malware scan, if the scan completed.

ScannedResourceArn.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the scanned resource.

ScannedResourceType.description: The resource type of the scanned resource.

ScanStatusReason.description: The reason for the scan status of this particular resource, if applicable.

EbsVolume.description: Contains information about the EBS volume that was scanned.

DeviceName.description: The device name for the EBS volume.

KmsKeyArn.description: KMS key ARN used to encrypt the EBS volume.

EbsSnapshot.description: Contains information about the EBS snapshot that was scanned.

DeviceName.description: The device name of the EBS snapshot that was scanned.

ScanResourceCriteria.description: Represents the criteria to be used in the filter for scanning resources.

EbsSnapshotPreservation.description: An enum value representing possible snapshot preservation settings.

Include.description: Represents condition that when matched will allow a malware scan for a certain resource.

Exclude.description: Represents condition that when matched will prevent a malware scan for a certain resource.

AccountId.description: The ID of the account used as the administrator account.

InvitationId.description: The value used to validate the administrator account to the member account.

RelationshipStatus.description: The status of the relationship between the administrator and member accounts.

InvitedAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was sent.

MemberDataSourceConfigurations.description: An object that describes which data sources are enabled for a member account.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of member account IDs that were unable to be processed along with an explanation for why they were not processed.

AccountId.description: The account ID for the member account.

DataSources.description: Contains information on the status of data sources for the account.

CloudTrail.description: An object that contains information on the status of CloudTrail as a data source.

Status.description: Describes whether CloudTrail is enabled as a data source for the detector.

DNSLogs.description: An object that contains information on the status of DNS logs as a data source.

Status.description: Denotes whether DNS logs is enabled as a data source.

FlowLogs.description: An object that contains information on the status of VPC flow logs as a data source.

Status.description: Denotes whether VPC flow logs is enabled as a data source.

S3Logs.description: An object that contains information on the status of S3 Data event logs as a data source.

Status.description: A value that describes whether S3 data event logs are automatically enabled for new members of the organization.

Kubernetes.description: An object that contains information on the status of all Kubernetes data sources.

AuditLogs.description: Describes whether Kubernetes audit logs are enabled as a data source.

Status.description: A value that describes whether Kubernetes audit logs are enabled as a data source.

MalwareProtection.description: Describes the configuration of Malware Protection data sources.

ScanEc2InstanceWithFindings.description: Describes the configuration of Malware Protection for EC2 instances with findings.

EbsVolumes.description: Describes the configuration of scanning EBS volumes as a data source.

Features.description: Contains information about the status of the features for the member account.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

DetectorId.description: The detector ID of the member account.

Email.description: The email address of the member account.

RelationshipStatus.description: The status of the relationship between the member and the administrator.

InvitedAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was sent.

Accounts.description: The member accounts which were included in a request and were processed successfully.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: The member account that was included in a request but for which the request could not be processed.

AccountId.description: The account identifier of the GuardDuty member account.

DataSources.description: Describes the data source enabled for the GuardDuty member account.

CloudTrail.description: Describes whether any Amazon Web Services CloudTrail management event logs are enabled as data sources.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

DnsLogs.description: Describes whether any DNS logs are enabled as data sources.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

FlowLogs.description: Describes whether any VPC Flow logs are enabled as data sources.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

S3Logs.description: Describes whether any S3 data event logs are enabled as data sources.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

Kubernetes.description: Describes whether any Kubernetes logs are enabled as data sources.

AuditLogs.description: Describes whether Kubernetes audit logs are enabled as a data source.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

MalwareProtection.description: Describes whether Malware Protection is enabled as a data source.

ScanEc2InstanceWithFindings.description: Describes whether Malware Protection for EC2 instances with findings is enabled as a data source.

FreeTrialDaysRemaining.description: A value that specifies the number of days left to use each enabled data source.

Features.description: A list of features enabled for the GuardDuty account.

Name.description: The name of the threat entity set associated with the specified threatEntitySetId.

Format.description: The format of the file that contains the threat entity set.

Location.description: The URI of the file that contains the threat entity set.

ExpectedBucketOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the Amazon S3 bucket specified in the location parameter.

Status.description: The status of the associated threat entity set.

Tags.description: The tags associated with the threat entity set resource.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp when the associated threat entity set was created.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the associated threat entity set was updated.

ErrorDetails.description: The error details when the status is shown as ERROR.

Name.description: A user-friendly ThreatIntelSet name displayed in all findings that are generated by activity that involves IP addresses included in this ThreatIntelSet.

Location.description: The URI of the file that contains the ThreatIntelSet.

Tags.description: The tags of the threat list resource.

ExpectedBucketOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the Amazon S3 bucket specified in the location parameter. This field appears in the response only if it was provided during ThreatIntelSet creation or update.

Name.description: The name of the threat entity set associated with the specified trustedEntitySetId.

Format.description: The format of the file that contains the trusted entity set.

Location.description: The URI of the file that contains the trusted entity set.

ExpectedBucketOwner.description: The Amazon Web Services account ID that owns the Amazon S3 bucket specified in the location parameter.

Status.description: The status of the associated trusted entity set.

Tags.description: The tags associated with trusted entity set resource.

CreatedAt.description: The timestamp when the associated trusted entity set was created.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp when the associated trusted entity set was updated.

ErrorDetails.description: The error details when the status is shown as ERROR.

UsageStatistics.description: The usage statistics object. If a UsageStatisticType was provided, the objects representing other types will be null.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

SumByAccount.description: The usage statistic sum organized by account ID.

TopAccountsByFeature.description:
Lists the top 50 accounts by feature that have generated the most GuardDuty usage, in the order from most to least expensive.

Currently, this doesn't support RDS_LOGIN_EVENTS.

SumByDataSource.description: The usage statistic sum organized by on data source.

SumByResource.description: The usage statistic sum organized by resource.

TopResources.description: Lists the top 50 resources that have generated the most GuardDuty usage, in order from most to least expensive.

SumByFeature.description: The usage statistic sum organized by feature.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

Resources.description: A list of resources and their attributes providing cluster details.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

DetectorId.description: The unique ID of the GuardDuty detector associated with the resource.

AccountId.description: The unique ID of the Amazon Web Services account.

ResourceDetails.description: Information about the resource for which the coverage statistics are retrieved.

EksClusterDetails.description: EKS cluster details involved in the coverage statistics.

CoveredNodes.description: Represents the nodes within the EKS cluster that have a HEALTHY coverage status.

CompatibleNodes.description: Represents all the nodes within the EKS cluster in your account.

ManagementType.description:
Indicates how the Amazon EKS add-on GuardDuty agent is managed for this EKS cluster.

AUTO_MANAGED indicates GuardDuty deploys and manages updates for this resource.

MANUAL indicates that you are responsible to deploy, update, and manage the Amazon EKS add-on GuardDuty agent for this resource.

ResourceType.description: The type of Amazon Web Services resource.

EcsClusterDetails.description: Information about the Amazon ECS cluster that is assessed for runtime coverage.

ClusterName.description: The name of the Amazon ECS cluster.

FargateDetails.description: Information about the Fargate details associated with the Amazon ECS cluster.

Issues.description: Runtime coverage issues identified for the resource running on Amazon Web Services Fargate.

ManagementType.description:
Indicates how the GuardDuty security agent is managed for this resource.

  • AUTO_MANAGED indicates that GuardDuty deploys and manages updates for this resource.

  • DISABLED indicates that the deployment of the GuardDuty security agent is disabled for this resource.

The MANUAL status doesn't apply to the Amazon Web Services Fargate (Amazon ECS only) woprkloads.

ContainerInstanceDetails.description: Information about the Amazon ECS container running on Amazon EC2 instance.

CoveredContainerInstances.description: Represents the nodes in the Amazon ECS cluster that has a HEALTHY coverage status.

CompatibleContainerInstances.description: Represents total number of nodes in the Amazon ECS cluster.

Ec2InstanceDetails.description: Information about the Amazon EC2 instance assessed for runtime coverage.

InstanceType.description: The instance type of the Amazon EC2 instance.

ClusterArn.description: The cluster ARN of the Amazon ECS cluster running on the Amazon EC2 instance.

Version.description: Version of the installed GuardDuty security agent.

ManagementType.description:
Indicates how the GuardDuty security agent is managed for this resource.

  • AUTO_MANAGED indicates that GuardDuty deploys and manages updates for this resource.

  • MANUAL indicates that you are responsible to deploy, update, and manage the GuardDuty security agent updates for this resource.

The DISABLED status doesn't apply to Amazon EC2 instances and Amazon EKS clusters.

CoverageStatus.description: Represents the status of the EKS cluster coverage.

Issue.description: Represents the reason why a coverage status was UNHEALTHY for the EKS cluster.

UpdatedAt.description: The timestamp at which the coverage details for the resource were last updated. This is in UTC format.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

FindingIds.description: The IDs of the findings that you're listing.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.
The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

Members.description:
A list of members.

The values for email and invitedAt are available only if the member accounts are added by invitation.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

DetectorId.description: The detector ID of the member account.

Email.description: The email address of the member account.

RelationshipStatus.description: The status of the relationship between the member and the administrator.

InvitedAt.description: The timestamp when the invitation was sent.

Destinations.description: A Destinations object that includes information about each publishing destination returned.

NextToken.description: A token to use for paginating results that are returned in the response. Set the value of this parameter to null for the first request to a list action. For subsequent calls, use the NextToken value returned from the previous request to continue listing results after the first page.

DestinationId.description: The unique ID of the publishing destination.

DestinationType.description: The type of resource used for the publishing destination. Currently, only Amazon S3 buckets are supported.

ThreatEntitySetIds.description: The IDs of the threat entity set resources.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.
The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

TrustedEntitySetIds.description: The IDs of the trusted entity set resources.

NextToken.description: The pagination parameter to be used on the next list operation to retrieve more items.

ScanId.description: A unique identifier that gets generated when you invoke the API without any error. Each malware scan has a corresponding scan ID. Using this scan ID, you can monitor the status of your malware scan.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain the unprocessed account and a result string that explains why it was unprocessed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of objects that contain an accountId for each account that could not be processed, and a result string that indicates why the account was not processed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.

UnprocessedAccounts.description: A list of member account IDs that were unable to be processed along with an explanation for why they were not processed.

Result.description: A reason why the account hasn't been processed.
